raw_consumer_statement:
On or about XXXX XXXX, XXXX  at approx. XXXX XXXX my wife and I were trying to cast from my android phone to our XXXX television. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX was the app. I downloading from the XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. After trying to get it to work for XXXX minutes, we gave up, cancelled the service and deleted the app. Before deleting the app I was given a notice that, " deleting this app. will result in the loss of this service ''. Since the service didn't work, deleting it would not be detrimental. 

I got billed {$29.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. When I received my credit card bill, I called to dispute the charge and American Express said I had to go to " XXXX XXXX '' to dispute the charge there. I could not find the app. on this site. 

American Express said I couldn't dispute the charge without proof from XXXX that I had cancelled the service under the free trial period. I never received anything from XXXX verifying the app. purchase or the cancellation. American Express told me then that I couldn't do anything without it. 

I can not be the only person that this has happened to. This appears to be blame on both XXXX and American Expresses part. I have spent over an hour of my time trying to get something done that I was not at fault. I feel I should be compensated for my time and XXXX and American Express should not be allowed to charge people this way with no recourse unless you are willing to devote hours of time to correct the situation. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
